description_long: This object is a ceremonial flag mounted on a dark green metal pole with a round base, designed for upright display. The flag itself is composed of two distinct designs: one side features a tricolor background with a large, detailed golden double-headed eagle crest, while the other side is predominantly red with white Cyrillic text and a hammer and sickle symbol, reminiscent of Soviet iconography. The pole is topped with a decorative finial, adding a formal touch to the display. The flag fabric appears to be slightly draped and gathered, suggesting a soft, flexible textile material. The base is sturdy and weighted, ensuring stability when placed on flat surfaces such as floors or podiums. This type of object is commonly found in government buildings, public squares, or at ceremonial events.
description: A ceremonial flag on a dark green metal pole with a round base, featuring a double-headed eagle crest on one side and Soviet-style red with Cyrillic text on the other. The flag is draped and mounted upright with a decorative finial.
description_view_0: The flag is shown with both the tricolor eagle crest and the red Soviet-style side partially visible, highlighting the contrast between the two designs.
description_view_1: The view focuses on the red side of the flag, displaying white Cyrillic text and the hammer and sickle symbol, with the pole and finial clearly visible.
description_view_2: The flag is seen from the back, with the red side more prominent and the tricolor side partially hidden, emphasizing the draped fabric and the round base.
description_view_3: The tricolor side with the golden eagle crest is most visible, while the red side is partially turned away, showing the flag's layered drape and the pole's structure.
attribute_graphic_design: One side features a tricolor background with a large golden double-headed eagle crest, while the other side is red with white Cyrillic text and a hammer and sickle symbol. The flag is visually divided and highly symbolic.
